'''Grodin Tierce''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as a [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guard]]. During the [[Thrawn campaign]], he was [[Grodin Tierce (clone)|cloned]] by the [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] as part of a new project to create super-commandos. He died shortly after. His clone was later involved in a campaign to bring down the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], before being killed in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]].

==Biography==
As with all Royal Guards, Tierce began his military career as a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtrooper]]. Under the designation '''TR-889''', Tierce ostensibly proved himself an exceptional soldier and was chosen for training as a Royal Guard on [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]].

[[File:Grodin_tierce.jpg|thumb|left|150px|[[Grodin Tierce (clone)|Grodin Tierce's clone]].]]

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Tierce was embedded in a Stormtrooper unit on [[Magagran]] battling a rebel cell. The periodic rotation of Royal Guards back into the [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] proved vital to Tierce when a large number of Royal Guards perished in the destruction of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]].

At some point after the death of [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], Tierce came to serve as a Stormtrooper officer under [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] aboard the Star Destroyer [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']]. Having impressed Thrawn with his martial skill and physical prowess, Thrawn chose Tierce to act as a template for a new type of [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] two months before the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Battle of Bilbringi]] in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]. Tierce felt responsible for Thrawn's death. He had placed most of his troops near the hangars of the ''Chimaera'', expecting that an attempt on Thrawn's life would come from a commando team landing in the hangars during a battle. Instead, Thrawn was killed by [[Rukh/Legends|Rukh]], after the [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] had learned of the Empire's treachery toward them. Tierce purportedly killed Rukh on his escape. Tierce died on [[Generis/Legends|Generis]] soon after.

{{Char-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
According to ''[[Specter of the Past]]'' and the [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry for [[Rukh/Legends|Rukh]], after he assassinated Thrawn, Tierce killed the Noghri. However, in ''[[Vision of the Future]]'', Tierce is said to have died during the assault on Generis that occurred prior to Thrawn's death. It was possible that his clone was the one who led the squad.

''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' misspells Tierce's first name as "Grodon."
